text.title: Documentation Antipatterns
text.summary: Over-documentation, hallucinated commands, missing gotchas, AGENTS.md drift, and secrets in docs.

antipattern.description: Writing 500-line AGENTS.md with full API reference, changelog, and architecture diagrams inline.
antipattern.reason: A 500-line AGENTS.md is loaded in full every time an agent enters the directory. Bulk context load negates the routing-first principle. Detail belongs in .agents/ reference docs.

antipattern.description: Writing npm test or pytest in Common Commands without verifying the commands exist in package.json or Makefile.
antipattern.reason: Agents execute commands from AGENTS.md verbatim. A hallucinated command fails silently or produces a misleading error that wastes investigation time.

antipattern.description: AGENTS.md with no Gotchas section because "there are no obvious gotchas."
antipattern.reason: Gotchas are always present; they are just not obvious from reading the directory. They live in TODO/FIXME comments and fix commit messages. Skipping the section guarantees agents reproduce known bugs.

antipattern.description: Source files updated but AGENTS.md not regenerated, causing stale file tables and outdated commands.
antipattern.reason: Agents route based on AGENTS.md. Stale tables send agents to deleted files or miss newly created entry points.

rule.statement: Run the audit-agents-md.sh drift detector before each release to flag directories where AGENTS.md is older than source files by more than 14 days.

antipattern.description: Including API keys, environment variable values, or references to .env file paths with secrets in AGENTS.md.
antipattern.reason: AGENTS.md is committed to the repo. Secrets leak via grep and log forwarding. Reference env var names only, never values.
